'''Kevin Wayne Jeter''' (born [[1950s|1950]]) is an American science fiction and horror author, who wrote ''[[The Bounty Hunter Wars]]'' trilogy. He is known for his literary writing style, dark themes, and paranoid, unsympathetic characters. He has also written several ''Star Trek: Deep Space Nine'' novels and comics set in the [[Wikipedia:Star Trek|Star Trek]] universe, and three sequels to [[Wikipedia:Blade Runner|''Blade Runner'']].

==Biography==
Jeter attended college at California State University, Fullerton where he became friends with [[Wikipedia:James P. Blaylock|James P. Blaylock]] and [[Wikipedia:Tim Powers|Tim Powers]], and through them, [[Wikipedia:Philip K. Dick|Philip K. Dick]]. Many of Jeter's books focus on the subjective nature of reality in a way that is reminiscent of the works of Dick.

Jeter wrote what was likely the first true cyberpunk novel (though he probably would protest that description), [[Wikipedia:Dr. Adder|''Dr. Adder'']], which was enthusiastically recommended by Philip K. Dick. Jeter also coined the term "[[Wikipedia:Steampunk|steampunk]]" to describe the retro-technology, alternate-history works that he published along with his friends Blaylock and Powers.

As well as his own original novels, K. W. Jeter has written a number of authorized novel sequels to the critically acclaimed 1982 motion picture ''Blade Runner'', which was adapted from Philip K. Dick's novel [[Wikipedia:Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep|''Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep'']].

He currently lives in Cuenca, Ecuador, with his wife, Geri.
